The purpose of this estimation study is to establish the uncertainty of of Phu My Gas Distribution
Centre (GDC). The uncertainty estimation has been performed at actual line condition then
transferred to the base condition al 15 'C and 1,01325 bar a. The uncertainty of the system will be
The cuslody transfer gas metering skid of Phu My GDC is Ultrasonic (USM) metering
Each systcm consists of two Ultrasonic flow metcrs, cach sized for 100% flow 5 000 in'/h.
connected in serics with bypass lines around them on a skid. In normal use, one of them is used as
the operation meter and the other as standby meter. Normally the flow shall be through the
operation meter and shall bypass the standby meter, by manually closing the bypass line. During
opcration meter maintenancc, the standby meter shall be used as the operation meter.
Each metcr run is equipped with one Daniel FloBoss S600 flow computer for flow
computation with built in pressure transmitter. Tempcrature transmitter is used for line gas
Series gas chromatograph (GC) is installed at the Phu My GDC main control terminal for line gas
through RS-485 serial link. Flow computcr shall use the received gas data and normalized it for
Line volume flowrate is calculated in accordance with AGA 9 for ultrasonic meter. Line
compressibility is calculated ini accordancc with AGA 8. Standard volume flow is then derived with
the PrZ formula and energy flow rale is obtained with the calorific value calculated using ISO
UNCERTAINTY ANALYSIS OF GAS METERING SYSTEM - PM4 POWER PLANT
The flow calculation methodologies for Phu My GDC - Gas metering system are in
accordance with AGA 9 for ultrasonic meter, AGA 8-1994, and ISO 6976-1995.
As refer to AGA 9, the flow process data measured by ultrasonic meter can be transmitted to
flow computer via MODBUS protocol through RS 485 serial link. In this project, a digital signal is
selected. The line volume flow rate is then given as below
